author: @mikeguitar9769
text: @0:35 It's interesting that impure urea in the form of urine has negative value, while pure urea is valuable enough to synthesize from natural gas.  Seems like we'd benefit from a practical process to purify urea because it's abundant in the impure form.  Seems like there are better uses for natural gas than to convert it to waste, lol.

author: @ClownWhisper
text:
I just tried this reaction and it did not work quite the same as above,  I used all standard chemicals,  my sulfuric acid is slightly higher than 18 mol wround 98.7% averaging three titrations."

When i started to add the sulfuric acid, 3 or 4 drops and three stirs perhaps it boiled over energetically similar to dumping sodium carbonate into acetic acid  It actually hardened in mid air as it overflowed.

now I will say that i stirred it on my hotplate and there was still heat in the plate from a precious reaction.  very low however. perhaps 35c maybe?  The solution was I would say 30c when I started to stir in the acid.

Would you think that I should dilute down to about a 95/96%?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Clown Whisper The acid is catalytic for the reaction - so yes lol - slow it down don't speed it up

replies.author: @ClownWhisper
replies.text:
Thank you robert.  I was not sure if it was the acid that was the culprit or the warmth of the solution.  FIgured I wouldn't  waste any more formaldehyde in an unintended homemade volcano until I asked the pro's :)

Thank you for the guidance mate.

now if I could only make delrin !!!

 .

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @DrCureAging
text:
Cool. Is there a name for the polymer produced? 
Since it's brittle, I'm wondering if I could mix it with a silicone to make it less brittle (I'm busy with exams so I can't try it right now) but I've been very interested with 3D printing. 
A kilo of ABS/PLA plastic costs about 20 dollars so I was actually looking into making very cheap plastic for 3D printers.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Nathaniel Zhu it's urea formaldehyde resin - it's probably one of the most produced plastics

replies.author: @Jononor
replies.text: Nathaniel Zhu urea formaldehyde resin is a thermosetting (as opposed to thermoplastic) plastic, so I don't think it will work for a FFF/FDM 3d-printer, which relies on being able to (re-)melt the plastic when squirted out the nozzle.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Jon Nordby it's cascamite glue mate - water soluble - don't know if this helps

replies.author: @Jononor
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith one could build a 3d-printer using a syringe-type extruder and extrude the plastic while wet, and let it cure as the printed shape. This could be interesting for medium scale fabrication, especially if mixing in some fibers (wood or similar). Then one essentially can 3d-print MDF/HDF. Likely the biggest challenges will be taking into account the shrinkage and non-uniform curing, as printed part may have different thickness in different places

replies.author: @Jononor
replies.text: The fact that curing will take a long time will also limit how tall objects can be built, as layers put on top will cause lower ones to deform if done while not cured

author: @ClownWhisper
text:
Whenever I make nitric acid now I actually dip my fingers in a strong base before I hand any of the glassware.  the stuff dips over the rim of any glassware and you do not want to get it on your fingers They will instantly turn a dark yellow the skin will fall off in about 4 days.  Nitric acid is actually a time where using gloves is a bad thing as it will destroy latex or nitrile instantly. keeping the fingertips damp right before going in to handle anything not only protects you to a degree but its the perfect alarm that you have come into contact with the acids as it will bubble like mad.


just a tip from a guy that had yellow pealing fingers one time too often.
So yes good tip on the keeping a base near by

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's urea - it's not a nitrophosphate - this is dried urine!! - if you want it and can't buy it - pee in a bucket and wait for it to dry

replies.author: @mikeguitar9769
replies.text:
Bit of an issue there because it seems that naturally occurring bacteria create enzymes that convert urea to ammonia.  This seems to be the cause of the strong odor of aged urine.  The effect would probably reduce the yield, but even if it didn't, it would be nice to avoid the effect of the odor.  So it may be appropriate to use some method of preservation, or else process the urine within 24 hours.  

Urea is more soluble than salt, so if you remove enough water, then the salt should precipitate out, leaving a concentrated urea solution.  Optional: To improve purity, dissolve in alcohol. (remaining salts are insoluble in alcohol).

author: @gugl4106
text: Would it be possible to do in any way without the sulfuric acid? Sulfuric acid is strictly regulated here in Norway.

replies.author: @Barskor1
replies.text: Hi Gugl You can make sulfuric acid really easy Do you have access to sulfur for gardening purposes? If so you can make a vaporizer to burn the sulfur in a acid proof chamber such a 2 litter soda bottle that is half filled with distilled water. the water will react with the sulfur fumes and make sulfuric acid. Concentrate the acid by evaporation or boiling slowly till white fumes appear always use safety gear and fume hood or work outside.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Barskor1
good post mate - thank you very much

replies.author: @gugl4106
replies.text: Sulfur is available. Thank you for answering.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: there are quite a few ways to cure urea formaldehyde resins - normally metal salts like aluminium sulphate are used but you can use ammonium chloride too - the cure time is much longer and you have to heat the resin for four hours in the make processes rather than just mixing it - so it is a bit more long winded - the version i saw is the demo you get at college when you do higher level chem  - it's a good demo because it is quick and easy - but in order to make it without the acid it is a bit more time consuming and difficult - hope that helps

replies.author: @Barskor1
replies.text: Gugl Your welcome
